findings.title: Unbounded `stakeWeek` lets a staker mint an arbitrarily large bonus and drain the pool

best_agent_match.title: Unbounded `stakeWeek` lets a staker mint an arbitrarily large bonus and drain the pool

findings.title: Reward emissions are underfunded because `rewardTokens` do not cover bonus liabilities

best_agent_match.title: Reward emissions are underfunded because `rewardTokens` do not cover bonus liabilities

findings.title: Owner can rug accrued user rewards through `rescueReward`

best_agent_match.title: Owner can rug accrued user rewards through `rescueReward`

rejected_candidates.title: Long-lock bonus is claimable immediately without completing the advertised lock
rejected_candidates.reason: Not clearly a distinct vulnerability from code alone. The contract formula pays a boosted reward stream while principal remains locked; harvesting before maturity changes payout timing, but the main reportable harm is already captured by the uncapped bonus and unfunded bonus-liability findings.

rejected_candidates.title: Double counting of pending rewards in unstake causes incorrect reward accounting
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. `rewardDebt += pending` happens only after the position is fully paid and marked unstaked, so it does not create a second claim path or protocol loss.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded token address with no validation
rejected_candidates.reason: Deployment/configuration choice, not an exploitable runtime flaw in the reviewed contract logic.

rejected_candidates.title: No mechanism to extend staking period
rejected_candidates.reason: Product/governance limitation only; no realistic fund-loss or denial-of-service scenario follows from the code alone.

rejected_candidates.title: Integer truncation in rewardPerSecond calculation causes precision loss
rejected_candidates.reason: At most leaves minor undistributed dust due to integer division. This is not material protocol harm.

rejected_candidates.title: No access control or validation on stakingCount access
rejected_candidates.reason: Nonexistent indices resolve to zeroed storage and are already harmlessly rejected by the `stakedAmount > 0` check.

rejected_candidates.title: No pausable mechanism for emergency stop
rejected_candidates.reason: Generic best-practice suggestion, not a concrete vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: No deadline validation for harvest after staking ends
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive. `_getMultiplier()` caps reward accrual at `stakingEndTime`, so harvesting after program end only claims already-accrued rewards.

rejected_candidates.title: startStaking is irreversible with no recovery mechanism
rejected_candidates.reason: Admin UX/governance limitation only; not a security issue by itself.
